member.name: T:LibreRally.BasicCameraController
member.summary: A script that allows to move and rotate an entity through keyboard, mouse and touch input to provide basic camera navigation.
member.remarks: The entity can be moved using W, A, S, D, Q and E, arrow keys, a gamepad's left stick or dragging/scaling using multi-touch. Rotation is achieved using the Numpad, the mouse while holding the right mouse button, a gamepad's right stick, or dragging using single-touch.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Camera.RallyCameraScript.BumperOffset
member.summary: Front bumper camera offset in car-local space (+Z = nose).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Camera.RallyCameraScript.ApplyCameraRotation(Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3)
member.summary: Points the camera toward using correct Stride conventions. Stride camera default forward = -Z. After RotationY(yaw), local -Z becomes (-sin yaw, 0, -cos yaw).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Camera.RallyCameraScript.UpdateCockpitCamera(Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3)
member.summary: Positions the camera at in car-local space, looking forward. Car nose = local +Z; Stride camera forward = local -Z, so we append a 180° Y rotation so that the camera's -Z aligns with the car's +Z (nose direction).

member.summary:
Loads all .jbeam files from a vehicle folder, resolves the slot hierarchy starting from the "main" slot, and produces a flat .
BeamNG jbeam coordinate space: X = right, Y = forward, Z = up. We convert to Stride space (X = right, Y = up, Z = backward) in rather than here, to preserve raw data fidelity.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.BreakablePartComponent
member.summary:
Monitors the weld constraint each frame and breaks it when the measured constraint force exceeds .
Once broken, the part's becomes a free rigid body that interacts normally with the physics world.
Attach this to the same entity as the and the part's .

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.BreakablePartComponent.BreakStrength
member.summary: Force threshold (N) above which the part detaches. Sourced from the minimum beamStrength of cross-boundary beams in the jbeam.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.BreakablePartComponent.WeldConstraint
member.summary: The weld constraint to break. Set by .

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.ChassisBodyRollSystem
member.summary:
Computes chassis body roll torque from left/right suspension compression differences.
For each axle the compression delta between left and right wheels generates a roll moment about the vehicle's longitudinal axis:
τ_roll = (compression_left − compression_right) × rollStiffness
The resulting torque is applied as an angular impulse to the chassis body each physics step, producing realistic body lean during cornering.
Design constraints:
Zero per-frame allocations — struct with no heap state.
Fixed-timestep safe — torque is converted to an impulse using the caller's dt.
Compatible with suspension compression arrays.
Reference: Milliken & Milliken, "Race Car Vehicle Dynamics", §17 — Roll stiffness and suspension geometry.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.ChassisBodyRollSystem.FrontRollStiffness
member.summary: Front axle roll stiffness (N·m/m). Torque per metre of left-right compression difference.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.ChassisBodyRollSystem.RearRollStiffness
member.summary: Rear axle roll stiffness (N·m/m). Torque per metre of left-right compression difference.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.ChassisBodyRollSystem.RollDampingCoefficient
member.summary: Viscous roll damping coefficient (N·m·s/rad). Resists rate of roll change to prevent oscillation.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.ChassisBodyRollSystem.Apply(Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single},System.Single)
member.summary:
Computes and applies chassis body roll torque from suspension compression differences.
Call once per physics step after suspension compressions have been measured and before tyre force application.
member.param: BEPU rigid body for the chassis.
Unit vector along the chassis longitudinal (forward) axis in world space.
Per-wheel suspension compression (m). Indexed by wheel constants.
Physics timestep in seconds.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialType
member.summary: Differential type enumeration. Each type distributes engine torque differently between the two output shafts. Reference: Milliken & Milliken, "Race Car Vehicle Dynamics", §6.4.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialType.Open
member.summary: Open differential: equal torque split, allows unlimited speed difference. The faster-spinning wheel limits available traction — poor for rally.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialType.LimitedSlip
member.summary: Limited-slip differential: transfers torque based on wheel speed difference up to a bias ratio. Improves traction on mixed-grip surfaces.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialType.Locking
member.summary: Locking differential: both wheels rotate at the same speed. Maximum traction on loose surfaces but reduces steering agility.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig
member.summary: Configuration for a single differential unit. Used for front, rear, or center differentials in an AWD drivetrain.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.Type
member.summary: Type of differential behaviour.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.BiasRatio
member.summary: Bias ratio for limited-slip differentials (typically 1.5–4.0). Defines the maximum torque ratio between the two output shafts: T_slow / T_fast ≤ biasRatio. A value of 1.0 = open; higher = more locking tendency. Reference: Milliken, RCVD §6.4.2.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.LockingCoefficient
member.summary: Locking coefficient for clutch-type LSDs (0.0–1.0). Controls how aggressively the LSD locks under acceleration. 0 = pure open behaviour, 1 = immediate full lock.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.CoastLockingCoefficient
member.summary: Locking coefficient used while coasting/engine braking (input torque < 0). If zero, is used for both directions.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.PreloadTorque
member.summary: Base clutch preload torque for limited-slip differentials (N·m). Applied as baseline locking before torque-proportional lock is added.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.CreateOpen
member.summary: Creates a default open differential.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.CreateLimitedSlip(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Creates a default limited-slip differential with typical rally bias.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig.CreateLocking
member.summary: Creates a locking differential.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialSolver
member.summary:
Stateless differential torque-split calculations.
Torque path: engine → clutch → gearbox → center diff → front/rear diff → wheels.
Each differential splits its input torque to two outputs based on wheel speed difference and the configured .
All methods are static and allocation-free for performance with 50+ vehicles.
Reference: Milliken & Milliken, "Race Car Vehicle Dynamics", Chapter 6.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialSolver.SplitTorque(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig@,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Splits into two output torques based on the differential configuration and the angular velocities of the two output shafts.
member.param: Differential configuration.
Total torque entering the differential (N·m).
Angular velocity of the left output shaft (rad/s).
Angular velocity of the right output shaft (rad/s).
Output: torque delivered to the left shaft (N·m).
Output: torque delivered to the right shaft (N·m).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialSolver.SplitOpen(System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Open differential: equal torque split (50/50). Each shaft receives half the input torque regardless of speed difference. This is the simplest and least effective for rally use.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialSolver.SplitLimitedSlip(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialConfig@,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary:
Limited-slip differential: transfers additional torque to the slower wheel based on speed difference and bias ratio.
Model: clutch-type LSD with preload. Locking torque uses a smooth speed-delta ramp via tanh and combines preload plus torque-proportional locking: T_lock = (T_preload + lockCoeff(mode) × |T_input|) × tanh(|Δω| / ω_ref) where mode uses drive lock for positive or zero input torque and coast lock for negative input torque. The locked torque is transferred from the faster to the slower wheel, clamped so the torque ratio does not exceed the bias ratio.
Reference: Milliken, RCVD §6.4.2, clutch-type LSD model.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.DifferentialSolver.SplitLocking(System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary: Locking differential: equal speed, equal torque. Both output shafts forced to rotate at the same speed (rigid coupling). For torque purposes this is equivalent to a 50/50 split since the wheel speed equalisation is handled by the constraint system.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceType
member.summary:
Identifies a driving surface material. Each type defines a unique set of friction, resistance, and deformation properties that modify tyre grip and slip behaviour.
Reference: Pacejka, "Tire and Vehicle Dynamics", Chapter 4 — road surface effects. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603 — road surface texture and skid resistance.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceType.WetTarmac
member.summary: Wet tarmac — reduced grip from surface water film.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties
member.summary:
Physics properties for a driving surface. These values scale the tyre model's grip envelope and energy dissipation.
Surface friction is decomposed into two texture-scale contributions following The Contact Patch, C1603:
: overall peak µ multiplier (1.0 = reference dry tarmac). This is the combined result of microtexture and macrotexture contributions.
: adhesion-dominant grip from sub-0.5 mm asperity peaks (0–1 scale). Provides dry and light-wet grip. Polished surfaces have low microtexture. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Microtexture.
: hysteresis grip and water-evacuation capacity from 0.5–20 mm scale aggregate texture (0–1 scale). High macrotexture drains water from the contact patch, delaying hydroplaning onset. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Macro-texture.
: surface water film thickness (m). Zero for dry surfaces. Values above ~0.0025 m (2.5 mm) trigger hydroplaning risk at speed. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Aqua-planing.
: rolling-resistance force coefficient (N per N of load).
: how much the surface deforms under load (0 = rigid, 1 = fully deformable). Affects longitudinal slip behaviour — deformable surfaces tolerate higher slip before saturation.
: road roughness amplitude (0 = smooth, 1 = very rough). Drives deterministic per-frame micro-variation in grip based on road-surface power spectral density, giving the feel of aggregate texture at the contact patch. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Power spectral density curves.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties.Microtexture
member.summary: Microtexture level (0–1). Represents sub-0.5 mm asperity peaks that provide adhesion grip. Higher values = grittier surface = better dry and light-wet grip. Fresh calcined bauxite ≈ 1.0; polished stone ≈ 0.3. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Microtexture.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties.Macrotexture
member.summary: Macrotexture level (0–1). Represents 0.5–20 mm aggregate texture depth. High macrotexture provides hysteresis grip and drains water from the contact patch. Positive texture (surface dressing) ≈ 0.9; polished concrete ≈ 0.2. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Macro-texture.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties.WaterDepth
member.summary: Surface water film thickness (m). Zero for dry conditions. Light rain ≈ 0.0005 m, moderate ≈ 0.001 m, heavy ≈ 0.003 m. Hydroplaning onset ≈ 0.0025 m at highway speeds. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Aqua-planing.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties.ForType(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceType)
member.summary: Returns the default for the given . Values are tuned for rally driving conditions. Friction coefficients calibrated from The Contact Patch, C1603, Table 1: dry tarmac 0.8–1.0, wet 0.2–0.65, icy 0.15. Microtexture/macrotexture values represent typical UK road aggregate.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState
member.summary:
Mutable per-wheel thermal and wear state. Updated every physics step by .
Temperature model reference: Salaani et al., "An Analytical Tire Model for Use in Vehicle Dynamics Simulations", SAE 2007-01-0816. Wear model: simplified abrasion proportional to slip energy dissipation.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.Temperature
member.summary: Current tyre surface temperature (°C). Starts at ambient (~30 °C).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.TreadLife
member.summary: Remaining tread fraction (1.0 = new, 0.0 = fully worn).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.AngularVelocity
member.summary: Wheel angular velocity (rad/s). Positive = forward rolling.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.LateralDeflection
member.summary: Lateral deflection state for the brush contact-patch model (m).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.LongitudinalDeflection
member.summary: Longitudinal deflection state for the brush contact-patch model (m).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.SlipRatio
member.summary: Current longitudinal slip ratio (dimensionless).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.SlipAngle
member.summary: Current lateral slip angle (rad).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState.CreateDefault
member.summary: Creates a fresh tyre state with ambient temperature, full tread life, and zero slip state.
member.returns: A new initialized to the model defaults.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel
member.summary:
Slip-based tyre physics model combining brush tyre theory with Pacejka-like force curves.
Slip ratio (longitudinal): κ = (ω·R − Vx) / max(|Vx|, ε) where ω = wheel angular velocity, R = tyre radius, Vx = longitudinal velocity.
Slip angle (lateral): α = atan(Vy / |Vx|) where Vy = lateral velocity at the contact patch.
Forces are computed using a simplified Pacejka "Magic Formula": F = D · sin(C · atan(B·x − E·(B·x − atan(B·x)))) with load-sensitive peak factor D = µ·Fz and appropriate stiffness B.
Key references:
Pacejka, "Tire and Vehicle Dynamics", 3rd Ed., Chapters 3–4.
Brush tyre model: Milliken & Milliken, "Race Car Vehicle Dynamics", §2.5.
Rally high-slip: Abdulrahim, "Measurement and Analysis of Rally Car Dynamics at High Attitude Angles", SAE 2007.
Designed for struct-based per-wheel state () to avoid per-frame heap allocations when simulating 50+ vehicles.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.Radius
member.summary: Unloaded tyre radius (m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.Width
member.summary: Tyre width (m), used for contact patch size estimation.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.TyrePressure
member.summary: Gets or sets the tyre inflation pressure in kilopascals. Higher values increase carcass stiffness and reduce the effective contact-patch size. Lower values increase compliance and usually improve low-speed grip.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.SidewallStiffness
member.summary: Gets or sets the sidewall stiffness multiplier, where 1.0 is the baseline response. Increasing this value sharpens turn-in by building lateral force more quickly. Decreasing this value makes the tyre feel softer and slower to respond.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.CarcassStiffness
member.summary: Gets or sets the carcass stiffness multiplier, where 1.0 is the baseline tyre-body compliance. Increasing this value reduces transient delay before forces build at the contact patch. Decreasing this value allows more carcass deformation and slower transient response.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ContactPatchLengthScale
member.summary: Contact patch length scale factor (dimensionless) applied to the theoretical rectangular footprint length computed from load, pressure, and width: length ≈ normalLoad / (pressure × width). Values above 1.0 lengthen the effective patch for extra compliance; values below 1.0 shorten it. Reference: The Contact Patch, "The contact patch"; Pacejka §3.2.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ContactPatchLength
member.summary: Obsolete alias for retained for backward compatibility. This value is a dimensionless scale factor, not an absolute length in metres.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.VerticalStiffness
member.summary: Baseline vertical stiffness of the tyre carcass (N/m) at the 220 kPa reference pressure. Used to estimate loaded-radius deflection and therefore effective rolling radius. Higher values reduce loaded deflection and slip generated by carcass compression.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WheelInertia
member.summary:
Wheel rotational inertia (kg·m²). Governs spin-up/spin-down response. Typical values: 0.5–1.5 kg·m² for passenger/rally tyres. When set to 0 (default), inertia is estimated from normal load and radius.
angularAccel = appliedTorque / WheelInertia Reference: Milliken, RCVD §2.3, wheel inertia.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LongitudinalB
member.summary: Longitudinal stiffness factor B (cornering stiffness / peak force).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LongitudinalC
member.summary: Longitudinal shape factor C. Typically 1.5–1.8 for combined slip models.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LongitudinalE
member.summary: Longitudinal curvature factor E. Negative values extend the curve past the peak.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LateralB
member.summary: Lateral stiffness factor B.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LateralC
member.summary: Lateral shape factor C. Typically 1.1–1.4 for passenger/rally tyres.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LateralE
member.summary: Lateral curvature factor E.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.FrictionEllipseRatio
member.summary: Ratio of lateral grip limit to longitudinal grip limit. 1.0 gives an equal-grip friction circle; values below 1.0 produce a friction ellipse.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.PeakFrictionCoefficient
member.summary: Peak friction coefficient on reference surface (tarmac).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LoadSensitivity
member.summary:
Load sensitivity exponent for the tyre's non-linear grip response to vertical load.
µ_effective = µ_peak × (Fz / Fz_ref)^(-LoadSensitivity) Higher values make grip fall away faster as load rises. Typical range 0.0–0.20; 0.15 is a representative road-tyre value.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.BeamNgNoLoadFrictionCoefficient
member.summary: BeamNG-style zero-load friction coefficient, when provided by JBeam tyre metadata.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.BeamNgFullLoadFrictionCoefficient
member.summary: BeamNG-style asymptotic full-load friction coefficient, when provided by JBeam tyre metadata.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.BeamNgLoadSensitivitySlope
member.summary: BeamNG-style exponential load-sensitivity slope (1/N), when provided by JBeam tyre metadata.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ReferenceLoad
member.summary: Reference vertical load for µ rating (N).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ContactAreaGripExponent
member.summary: Gets or sets the exponent used for contact-patch-area grip scaling. Larger values make grip more sensitive to contact-patch area changes under load. Smaller values keep grip closer to the baseline friction curve.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ContactPatchStiffness
member.summary: Brush stiffness calibration (N/m at 220 kPa and 205 mm width). The effective brush stiffness scales from the pressure-membrane estimate pressure × width.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ContactPatchDamping
member.summary: Lateral contact-patch damping (N·s/m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.RelaxationLength
member.summary: Lateral relaxation length (m) for the brush tyre transient model.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.LongitudinalRelaxationLength
member.summary: Longitudinal relaxation length (m) for the brush tyre transient model. Typically shorter than lateral because the tyre carcass is stiffer in the rolling direction. Default 0.4 m. Reference: Pacejka §5.4; brush model braking transient.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.CamberThrustCoefficient
member.summary: Camber thrust coefficient (N per rad of camber per N of Fz).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.PneumaticTrail
member.summary: Pneumatic trail at zero slip (m). Mz = trail × Fy. Decreases with slip angle.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ThermalMass
member.summary: Thermal mass of the tyre surface (J/°C). Higher = slower temp change.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.CoolingRate
member.summary: Cooling rate to ambient (W/°C).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.AmbientTemperature
member.summary: Ambient temperature (°C).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.OptimalTemperature
member.summary: Optimal tyre temperature for peak grip (°C).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.TemperatureWindow
member.summary: Temperature window width (°C). Grip falls off outside [optimal ± window].

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WearRate
member.summary: Wear rate: tread life lost per unit of slip energy (1 / J).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WornGripFraction
member.summary: Minimum grip multiplier when tyre is completely worn (fraction of peak).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HighSlipForceRetention
member.summary: Fraction of peak lateral force retained at extreme slip angles (30–40°+). Rally tyres maintain ~60–70% of peak force at large slip to allow controlled drifts. Reference: Abdulrahim, "Measurement and Analysis of Rally Car Dynamics at High Attitude Angles".

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HighSlipTransitionStart
member.summary: Slip angle (rad) at which the rally high-slip retention curve begins blending. Below this angle the standard Pacejka curve applies fully. ~15° by default. Adjust per surface type or tyre compound for different drift-entry behaviour.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HighSlipTransitionEnd
member.summary: Slip angle (rad) at which the rally high-slip retention curve is fully active. ~40° by default.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.RollingResistanceCoefficient
member.summary: Base rolling-resistance coefficient (dimensionless). Typical 0.01–0.015 for tarmac tyres.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.StandingWaveCriticalSpeed
member.summary: Approximate carcass wave speed (m/s) above which standing-wave losses grow rapidly. Lower values increase high-speed rolling resistance sooner.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.StandingWaveResistanceGain
member.summary: Gain applied to the standing-wave rolling-resistance rise. Effective rolling resistance scales as 1 + gain × (speed / criticalSpeed)².

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.CarcassShearCoefficient
member.summary: Scaling factor for free-rolling carcass shear resistance. The carcass shear contribution arises from the difference between tread-element speed (Ω·r) and road speed (Ω·rₑ) as the tread compresses through the contact patch. Reference: "A simple model for tyre deformation", The Contact Patch, §Free rolling, Eq. 8.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.InertiaScalar
member.summary: Approximate wheel rotational inertia scalar. Wheel inertia ≈ effective_mass × R² × scalar. The 1.2 factor accounts for the distribution of tyre mass (annular ring rather than point mass). Typical road-tyre I ≈ 0.5–1.5 kg·m²; this scalar yields values in that range. Reference: Milliken, RCVD §2.3, approximate wheel inertia.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ReferencePressure
member.summary: Reference tyre pressure (kPa) used to normalise pressure-dependent effects. 220 kPa ≈ 32 psi, a typical rally tarmac cold-start pressure.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WetGripReferenceDepth
member.summary: Reference water depth (m) for normalizing wet grip loss. 4 mm = continuous sheet of water above asperity peaks.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WetGripBaseLoss
member.summary: Maximum base grip loss fraction from a water film at reference depth. Typical dry→wet friction ratio: 0.55/1.0 = 0.45 loss at worst. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, Table 1.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.WetGripDrainageRecovery
member.summary: Fraction of base wet grip loss recovered by full macrotexture drainage (0–1). High macrotexture surfaces (positive texture, porous asphalt) drain water from the contact patch and retain more adhesion grip. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Macro-texture.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HydroplaningSpeedConstant
member.summary: Hydroplaning speed constant: V_hydro = C / √(depth_m). Calibrated so that at 2.5 mm depth, V_hydro ≈ 22 m/s (80 km/h). C = 22 × √(0.0025) = 22 × 0.05 ≈ 1.1. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Aqua-planing.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HydroplaningOnsetFraction
member.summary: Fraction of V_hydro at which grip reduction begins ramping. Onset at 70% of critical speed gives a smooth transition zone.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HydroplaningMacrotextureBoost
member.summary: Boost to effective hydroplaning speed from surface macrotexture. Full macrotexture raises V_hydro by 30% (better drainage delays onset).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.HydroplaningMinGrip
member.summary: Minimum grip fraction during full hydroplaning. Even at full aquaplaning, a small residual grip remains from hysteresis.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.RoadNoiseGripAmplitude
member.summary: Maximum grip perturbation amplitude from road noise at full NoiseFactor. ±8% variation provides realistic road-surface PSD feel without destabilizing physics. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Statistical modelling of the road surface profile.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.#ctor(System.Single)
member.summary: Initializes a tyre model for a wheel radius.
member.param: Unloaded tyre radius in metres.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.Update(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreState@,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties@,System.Single,System.Single@,System.Single@,System.Single@)
member.summary:
Computes all tyre forces and torques for one wheel for one physics step.
Outputs:
: Fx along wheel heading (N). Positive = forward.
: Fy perpendicular to heading (N). Positive = rightward.
: Mz about the vertical axis (N·m). Opposes steering.
member.param: Per-wheel mutable state (angular velocity, temperature, wear, deflection).
Forward velocity at the contact patch (m/s).
Lateral velocity at the contact patch (m/s). Positive = rightward.
Vertical load on the tyre (N). Must be > 0 for contact.
Torque applied to the wheel from the drivetrain (N·m).
Braking torque magnitude (N·m). Always opposes rotation.
Camber angle (rad). Positive = top of wheel tilted outward.
Surface material properties affecting grip.
Physics timestep (s).
Output: longitudinal force (N).
Output: lateral force (N).
Output: self-aligning torque (N·m).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeEffectiveFriction(System.Single,LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.SurfaceProperties@,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Computes effective friction coefficient accounting for surface, load sensitivity, temperature window, tread wear, wet grip reduction, hydroplaning, microtexture/macrotexture, and road roughness noise.
Wet grip model (three-zone contact patch): Water on the road creates a leading wedge that lifts the tyre tread. Macrotexture drains water from the contact patch; microtexture penetrates the remaining thin film to restore adhesion. At higher speeds or deeper water, the drainage capacity is overwhelmed and grip collapses toward hydroplaning. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Dispersal of the water film, §Aqua-planing.
Hydroplaning onset: Grip collapses above a critical speed V_hydro ∝ 1/√(waterDepth). At 2.5 mm film depth, V_hydro ≈ 80 km/h (22 m/s). The transition uses a smooth squared ramp to avoid discontinuities. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Aqua-planing; O'Flaherty, film depth ≈ 2.5 mm.
Road roughness (NoiseFactor): A deterministic per-frame micro-variation in µ derived from speed and a low-frequency hash, modelling road-surface PSD excitation at the contact patch. Amplitude scales with NoiseFactor; frequency increases with speed. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Power spectral density curves.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeWetGripFactor(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Computes the wet grip reduction factor from water film depth, surface macrotexture, and vehicle speed. Includes hydroplaning onset.
The model has two components:
Base wet grip loss: thin film reduces adhesion grip by ~30–50%. Macrotexture drainage capacity mitigates this loss. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, Table 1 (wet µ ≈ 0.2–0.65 vs dry 0.8–1.0).
Speed-dependent hydroplaning: above a critical speed V_hydro, the leading water wedge lifts the tyre and grip collapses. V_hydro = HydroplaningSpeedConstant / √(waterDepth). Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Aqua-planing.
member.param: Surface water film thickness (m).
Surface macrotexture level (0–1).
Absolute longitudinal speed at the contact patch (m/s).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeRoadNoiseGripFactor(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Computes a deterministic grip micro-variation factor from road roughness (NoiseFactor). Models the effect of road-surface PSD on instantaneous grip at the contact patch. Uses a fast integer hash (Murmur-style finalizer) of a distance-proportional phase counter. Reference: The Contact Patch, C1603, §Power spectral density curves.
member.param: Road roughness amplitude (0–1).
Absolute longitudinal speed (m/s).
Physics timestep for this update (s).
member.returns: Grip multiplier in range [1 − noiseFactor × 0.08, 1 + noiseFactor × 0.08].

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel._noisePhase
member.summary: Accumulated phase for road noise grip perturbation. ThreadStatic so each physics thread maintains independent state without contention. Intentionally non-deterministic across threads — the noise represents stochastic road-surface PSD variation rather than a reproducible simulation input.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeAdhesionFraction(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Computes the adhesion fraction λ of the contact patch for a given slip input. λ = 1 means full adhesion (low slip); λ → 0 means nearly full sliding.
The slip threshold is µP / (4a²c).
When |slip| ≤ threshold, λ = 1 (pure adhesion).
When |slip| > threshold, λ = threshold / |slip| (mixed). Reference: The Contact Patch, C2015, Eq. 15.
member.param: Absolute value of the slip input (tan(α) for lateral, K for longitudinal).
µ · Fz — the maximum friction force (N).
Contact patch half-length a (m).
Brush stiffness per unit length of tread (N/m²).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeBrushPneumaticTrail(System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Computes the pneumatic trail from the brush-model adhesion fraction λ.
Pure adhesion (λ = 1): trail = a / 3.
Mixed adhesion/slip (λ < 1): trail = a · λ · (1 − 2λ/3) / (2 · (1 − λ/2)). Reference: The Contact Patch, C2015, Eq. 13, 20.
member.param: Contact patch half-length a (m).
Adhesion fraction (0–1).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.ComputeCarcassShearForce(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Computes the free-rolling carcass shear resistance force magnitude. Arises from the difference between unloaded radius and effective rolling radius. Reference: The Contact Patch, C2015, §Free rolling, Eq. 7–8.
member.param: r − rₑ (m). Positive when tyre is deflected.
Contact patch half-length a (m).
Brush stiffness per unit length of tread (N/m²).
Full contact patch length 2a (m).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.MagicFormula(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Pacejka Magic Formula: F = D · sin(C · atan(B·x − E·(B·x − atan(B·x)))). Reference: Pacejka, "Tire and Vehicle Dynamics", Eq. 4.6.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.TyreModel.MagicFormulaRally(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Modified Magic Formula for lateral force with rally high-slip behaviour.
Standard Pacejka Fy drops off sharply past the peak slip angle, which causes snap oversteer. Rally tyres maintain ~60-70% of peak force at large slip angles (20-40°+), enabling controlled drifts.
This implementation blends the normal Pacejka curve with a sustained force floor at high slip angles, referenced from: Abdulrahim, "Measurement and Analysis of Rally Car Dynamics at High Attitude Angles".

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem
member.summary:
Central vehicle dynamics coordinator.
Computes all vehicle forces each frame and applies them as impulses to BEPU bodies. BEPU remains responsible for rigid-body integration and collision; this system calculates tyre forces, load transfer, drivetrain torque, suspension reactions, and anti-roll bar effects externally.
Per-frame update order:
Compute longitudinal and lateral load transfer from chassis acceleration.
Compute anti-roll bar forces from suspension compression difference.
Apply chassis body roll torque from suspension compression difference.
Split engine torque through the drivetrain (center diff → axle diffs → wheels).
Evaluate tyre model for each wheel (slip ratio, slip angle → Fx, Fy, Mz).
Apply all computed forces/impulses to BEPU bodies.
Designed for 50+ simultaneous vehicles: uses fixed-size arrays, no per-frame allocations, struct-based per-wheel state.
Key references:
Pacejka, "Tire and Vehicle Dynamics", 3rd Ed.
Milliken & Milliken, "Race Car Vehicle Dynamics".
Abdulrahim, "Measurement and Analysis of Rally Car Dynamics at High Attitude Angles".

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.VehicleMass
member.summary: Total vehicle mass including chassis + wheels (kg).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.CgHeight
member.summary: Centre-of-gravity height above ground (m). Affects load transfer magnitude.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.Wheelbase
member.summary: Front-to-rear axle distance (m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.TrackWidth
member.summary: Left-to-right wheel centre distance (m). Used for lateral load transfer.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.FrontAntiRollStiffness
member.summary: Front anti-roll bar stiffness (N/m of compression difference). 0 = disabled.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.RearAntiRollStiffness
member.summary: Rear anti-roll bar stiffness (N/m of compression difference). 0 = disabled.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.BodyRoll
member.summary: Chassis body roll system. Generates roll torque from left/right suspension compression difference and applies it as an angular impulse to the chassis.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.FrontDiff
member.summary: Front axle differential configuration.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.RearDiff
member.summary: Rear axle differential configuration.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.CenterDiff
member.summary: Center differential configuration (AWD torque split front/rear).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.DriveFrontAxle
member.summary: Whether the front axle receives drive torque.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.DriveRearAxle
member.summary: Whether the rear axle receives drive torque.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.WheelStates
member.summary: Mutable per-wheel tyre state (temperature, wear, angular velocity, deflection).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.TyreModels
member.summary: Tyre model for each wheel (may share instances if identical).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.WheelSurfaces
member.summary: Surface properties currently under each wheel.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.StaticNormalLoads
member.summary: Static normal load per wheel (N), set during vehicle build.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.CurrentNormalLoads
member.summary: Current normal load per wheel after load transfer (N). Read by telemetry.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.SuspensionCompression
member.summary: Signed suspension travel per wheel (m). Positive = compressed, negative = rebound.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.WheelGrounded
member.summary: Whether each wheel currently has ground contact.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.LongitudinalForces
member.summary: Longitudinal force per wheel (N). Read by telemetry.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.LateralForces
member.summary: Lateral force per wheel (N). Read by telemetry.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.SelfAligningTorques
member.summary: Self-aligning torque per wheel (N·m). Applied to steering system.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.WheelDriveTorques
member.summary: Drive torque delivered to each wheel after differential (N·m).

Main per-frame update. Computes all vehicle dynamics and applies impulses to BEPU bodies.
Called from after input processing and motor commands.
member.param: BEPU rigid body for the chassis.
Chassis world transform matrix.
World-space position of each wheel contact point.
World-space linear velocity at each wheel.
World-space orientation frames for each wheel (Right, Up, Forward).
Per-wheel contact confidence/load scales in the range [0, 1].
Whether each wheel has ground contact.
Signed suspension travel for each wheel (m). Positive = compressed.
Total engine torque after gearbox at wheel level (N·m).
Brake torque per wheel (N·m).
Camber angle per wheel (rad).
Physics timestep (s).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.ComputeLoadTransfer(System.Single,System.Single,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Boolean},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single})
member.summary:
Computes longitudinal and lateral load transfer and modifies per-wheel normal loads.
Longitudinal load transfer (acceleration/braking): ΔF = (m · a_x · h_cg) / L Positive a_x (acceleration) shifts load rearward. Reference: Milliken, RCVD §18.2, Eq. 18.1.
Lateral load transfer (cornering): ΔF = (m · a_y · h_cg) / T Positive a_y (rightward) shifts load to the left wheels. Reference: Milliken, RCVD §18.3.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.ComputeAntiRollForces(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single})
member.summary:
Anti-roll bar simulation: links left/right suspension to resist body roll.
For each axle, computes the compression difference between left and right: δ = compression_left − compression_right F_roll = δ × K_arb This force is added to the inner wheel and subtracted from the outer, increasing load on the more-compressed side.
Anti-roll stiffness should be loaded from JBeam if available.
Reference: Milliken, RCVD §17.6, anti-roll bar mechanics.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.ComputeDrivetrainTorque(System.Single)
member.summary:
Distributes engine torque through the drivetrain: engine → clutch → gearbox → center differential → front/rear differential → wheels.
The center diff splits torque between front and rear axles. Each axle diff then splits between left and right wheels.
Reference: Milliken, RCVD Chapter 6.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.ComputeTyreForces(System.ReadOnlySpan{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Matrix},System.ReadOnlySpan{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Single},System.Single)
member.summary: Evaluates the tyre model for each wheel, computing longitudinal force (Fx), lateral force (Fy), and self-aligning torque (Mz).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.ApplyForces(Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Matrix@,System.ReadOnlySpan{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3},System.ReadOnlySpan{Stride.Core.Mathematics.Matrix},System.Single)
member.summary: Applies all computed tyre forces to the chassis at each wheel position. The wheel rigid bodies provide collision/suspension geometry, while the tyre model is authoritative for contact-patch grip and therefore pushes the sprung mass directly.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleDynamicsSystem.GetTotalSelfAligningTorque
member.summary: Returns the total self-aligning torque from all wheels. This should be fed back to the steering system to provide force feedback.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehiclePhysicsBuilder
member.summary: Builds the Stride entity graph and BEPU physics bodies for an assembled vehicle definition, including the chassis body and four wheel bodies connected by suspension constraints.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehiclePhysicsBuilder.Build(LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition)
member.summary: Creates a root entity containing: - A chassis entity with a and compound box colliders. - One entity per detachable part, each with a and a connecting it to the chassis. - Four wheel entities with suspension constraints.
member.param: Assembled vehicle definition containing nodes, parts, and beam metadata.
member.returns: A containing the created entities and chassis body.
member.exception: Thrown when the vehicle definition does not contain a non-detachable chassis part.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehiclePhysicsBuilder.ComputeWheelCenter(System.Collections.Generic.List{LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledNode},System.Single)
member.summary: Computes wheel centre from jbeam hub nodes (fallback when no flexbody pos is available). Nodes with Z < 0.10 must already be filtered out before calling this. Uses the most laterally-outer node for X, the mean of all filtered nodes for Y, and the minimum node Z (hub lower ball joint) + a fixed spindle offset for Z.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehiclePhysicsBuilder.BeamNGToStride(System.Numerics.Vector3)
member.summary: Converts a BeamNG-space vector to Stride-space coordinates.
member.param: Vector expressed in BeamNG coordinates (X right, Y forward, Z up).
member.returns: Equivalent vector in Stride coordinates (X right, Y up, Z backward).

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings
member.summary: Holds wheel constraint references and metadata so can drive throttle, brake, steering, and suspension-travel measurement each frame. Also stores references to the tyre model and dynamics system.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.DriveMotor
member.summary: Drives wheel spin in chassis-local space so the axle can steer without losing drive authority.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SteerMotor
member.summary: Steers the wheel around the chassis-up axis (front wheels only).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.CurrentSteerAngle
member.summary: Rate-limited rack command (radians) that the steering servo tracks.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.TyreModel
member.summary: Slip-based tyre model with Pacejka/brush physics for this wheel.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.StaticNormalLoad
member.summary: Nominal wheel load for the tyre model, initialized during vehicle build from quarter-mass × gravity.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionLocalOffsetA
member.summary: Chassis-local suspension anchor used by the wheel's linear suspension constraints.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionLocalOffsetB
member.summary: Wheel-local suspension anchor used by the wheel's linear suspension constraints.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionLocalAxis
member.summary: Chassis-local suspension axis. Positive travel is bump/compression; negative is rebound.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionTargetOffset
member.summary: Neutral suspension offset target in metres for the spring servo.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionMinimumOffset
member.summary: Minimum allowed suspension offset (rebound stop) in metres.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.SuspensionMaximumOffset
member.summary: Maximum allowed suspension offset (bump stop) in metres.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.DynamicsIndex
member.summary: Index of this wheel in the arrays (0=FL, 1=FR, 2=RL, 3=RR).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.CurrentSurface
member.summary: Current surface type under this wheel. Updated by raycast/contact detection.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.WheelSettings.GroundProbeHits
member.summary: Reusable hit buffer for the tyre ground-contact probe.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.IdleHeatFraction
member.summary: Fraction of heat generated at idle (no throttle).

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.OilViscosityTempCoef
member.summary: Oil viscosity coefficient: pressure drops by this fraction per °C above reference.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.OilViscosityRefTempC
member.summary: Oil reference temperature (°C) above which pressure starts to drop.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.Dynamics
member.summary: Vehicle dynamics system computing tyre forces, load transfer, differentials, and anti-roll bar effects. Created during and updated each frame.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.WheelRadius
member.summary: Wheel radius used to convert speed to spin rate (m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.BrakeMotorForce
member.summary: Brake motor force — how hard wheels resist rotation when braking.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.HandbrakeForceMultiplier
member.summary: Extra rear brake force multiplier for the handbrake so it can lock the rear axle.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.MaxSteerAngle
member.summary: Maximum steering wheel lock angle (radians). ~0.52 rad ≈ 30°.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.SteerRate
member.summary: Angular rate at which front wheels steer (rad/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.ChassisYawAssist
member.summary: Minimal parking-speed yaw helper. It only tops up initial rotation until tyre forces and suspension load transfer take over.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.FrontStaticCamberRadians
member.summary: Static front-axle camber angle (rad), sourced from JBeam alignment settings.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.RearStaticCamberRadians
member.summary: Static rear-axle camber angle (rad), sourced from JBeam alignment settings.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.FrontCamberGainPerMeter
member.summary: Front camber gain in bump/rebound (rad/m). Negative values add negative camber in bump.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.RearCamberGainPerMeter
member.summary: Rear camber gain in bump/rebound (rad/m). Negative values add negative camber in bump.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.LateralGrip
member.summary: Legacy grip tuning value, now used as the soft-body tyre model's low-speed damping gain.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.GearRatios
member.summary: Forward gear ratios indexed 1–N (index 0 = reverse).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.FinalDrive
member.summary: Final drive (differential) ratio.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TorqueCurveRpm
member.summary: RPM axis of the engine torque curve.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TorqueCurveNm
member.summary: Torque (N·m at crank) corresponding to each RPM in .

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.EngineInertia
member.summary: Engine + flywheel rotational inertia (kg·m²). Governs rev-response speed.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.EngineFriction
member.summary: Constant engine friction torque (N·m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.EngineDynamicFriction
member.summary: Friction term proportional to engine angular velocity (N·m per rad/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.EngineBrakeTorque
member.summary: Engine braking torque applied when throttle is lifted (N·m).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.MaxRpm
member.summary: Max engine RPM (redline).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.IdleRpm
member.summary: Idle RPM (minimum engine speed).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AutoClutchLaunchRpm
member.summary: Auto-clutch launch hold RPM used to let the engine build torque from a stop.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AutoClutchWheelspinWindowRpm
member.summary: Equivalent engine-RPM window of excess driven-wheel spin over which the automatic clutch reduces transmitted torque to stop a full-throttle launch becoming a sustained burnout.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AutoClutchMinTorqueScale
member.summary: Minimum fraction of engine torque the automatic clutch still transmits while limiting excessive grounded wheelspin.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlEnabled
member.summary: Enables engine-side traction control based on driven-wheel wheelspin.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlSlipRatioTarget
member.summary: Driven-wheel slip ratio target sourced from the active BeamNG traction-control definition.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlSlipRatioWindow
member.summary: Slip-ratio window above the TCS target over which torque reduction reaches the minimum scale.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlMinimumSpeedKmh
member.summary: TCS stays inactive right at crawl speed where slip-ratio estimates are numerically noisy.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlApplyRate
member.summary: How quickly TCS removes torque once excess driven-wheel slip is detected (1/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlReleaseRate
member.summary: How quickly TCS restores torque after wheel slip recovers (1/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionControlMinTorqueScale
member.summary: Minimum fraction of engine torque that TCS still allows through when fully active.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TractionLossSlipThreshold
member.summary: Driven-wheel slip ratio above which the HUD traction warning lamp turns on.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsEnabled
member.summary: Whether the active vehicle definition equips ABS.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.PlayerInputEnabled
member.summary: Allows UI systems to temporarily block player input without pausing physics.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsSlipRatioTarget
member.summary: Target braking slip-ratio magnitude taken from the active JBeam brake definition.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsSlipRatioWindow
member.summary: Slip-ratio window above the ABS target over which brake torque ramps down to the minimum scale.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsMinBrakeTorqueScale
member.summary: Minimum fraction of service-brake torque ABS still allows when a wheel is close to locking.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsMinimumSpeedKmh
member.summary: ABS stays inactive at parking speeds to avoid chatter right at a stop.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsApplyRate
member.summary: How quickly ABS removes brake torque once lock-up is detected (1/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.AbsReleaseRate
member.summary: How quickly ABS restores brake torque after wheel speed recovers (1/s).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.ShiftUpRpm
member.summary: RPM to upshift at (auto-transmission).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.ShiftDownRpm
member.summary: RPM to downshift at (auto-transmission).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.ShiftSlipAllowanceRpm
member.summary: Maximum extra RPM that driven-wheel slip may contribute above road-speed RPM for shifting and torque coupling.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.ReverseEngageSpeedKmh
member.summary: Vehicle speed below which holding brake can select reverse gear.

member.summary: Fuel tank capacity (litres). Zero when no tank is defined.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.StartingFuelLiters
member.summary: Initial fuel load (litres), resolved from JBeam startingFuelCapacity.

member.summary: Engine oil volume (litres) from JBeam. Used for thermal mass calculation.

member.summary: Engine block temperature at which damage begins (°C).

member.summary: Thermostat target temperature (°C) — the engine's normal operating temperature.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.EngineBlockAirCoolingEfficiency
member.summary: Air cooling efficiency coefficient from JBeam.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.BurnEfficiencyThrottle
member.summary: Burn efficiency throttle axis (0–1). Pair with .

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.BurnEfficiencyValues
member.summary: Burn efficiency values (0–1) corresponding to each throttle point.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.HasTurbo
member.summary: Whether this engine has a turbocharger.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TurboMaxBoostPsi
member.summary: Maximum turbo boost pressure in PSI (from wastegate setting).

member.summary: Current engine block temperature (°C). Settles at the air-regulator setpoint.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.FuelLiters
member.summary: Remaining fuel (litres).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.OilPressure
member.summary: Current oil pressure (bar). Correlates with RPM and oil volume.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.OilTemp
member.summary: Current oil temperature (°C). Lags slightly behind engine block temp.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.TurboBoostBar
member.summary: Current turbo boost pressure (bar). Zero when no turbo is present.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.GatherWheelData(Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Matrix)
member.summary: Gathers per-wheel position, velocity, orientation, and ground contact data into cached arrays for the .

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.InterpolateTorqueCurve(System.Single)
member.summary: Linearly interpolates the engine torque curve at the given RPM. Falls back to the sunburst 2.0T peak-torque value when no curve is configured.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.UpdateEngineThermals(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Lightweight per-frame engine thermal, fuel consumption, oil, and turbo simulation. Produces gauge-plausible values from JBeam engine/fuel-tank data without full thermo-fluid modelling.

member.name: F:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.PsiToBar
member.summary: PSI to bar conversion factor.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.RallyCarComponent.InterpolateBurnEfficiency(System.Single)
member.summary: Interpolates the burn efficiency curve at the given throttle fraction. Returns a default of 0.3 when no curve is defined.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.BeamNGMaterialLoader
member.summary: Loads BeamNG *.materials.json files and resolves material identifiers to concrete diffuse texture file paths so imported meshes can bind textures at runtime.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.BeamNGMaterialLoader.LoadMaterialTextures(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Scans every *.materials.json in and builds a map from material name → absolute path of the best diffuse texture.
is the parent directory of all vehicle folders, e.g. the "BeamNG Vehicles" directory. It is used to resolve /vehicles/… paths.
member.param: Folder containing the target vehicle's material definition files.
Root vehicles directory used to resolve BeamNG virtual texture paths.
member.returns: A dictionary keyed by material name, where each value is the absolute path of the resolved base-color texture.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.BeamNGMaterialLoader.LoadMaterialTextures(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Func{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Loads material texture mappings from one or more search folders.
member.param: Candidate directories to scan for *.materials.json files.
One or more BeamNG vehicles root directories used to resolve virtual /vehicles/... texture paths.
Optional callback that resolves virtual BeamNG paths when the texture is not found directly on disk.
member.returns: A dictionary keyed by material name, where each value is the first resolved base-color texture path. When duplicate material names are discovered, the existing mapping is kept and later matches are ignored.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.BeamNGMaterialLoader.FindBestTexturePath(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Returns the first non-null diffuse/baseColor texture path found in any stage, skipping placeholder textures. Returns if none are found.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.BeamNGMaterialLoader.ResolveVehiclePath(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.String},System.Func{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Converts a BeamNG virtual path like "/vehicles/RLA_Evo/Textures/carpet.dds" to an absolute file system path using as the mount point(s) for the /vehicles/ prefix. Returns null if the resolved file does not exist.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaVertex
member.summary: Vertex with position, normal, and UV coordinates.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaVertex.#ctor(System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Numerics.Vector2)
member.summary: Vertex with position, normal, and UV coordinates.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaMesh
member.summary: A single mesh sub-object from a Collada file, identified by geometry name.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaLoader
member.summary: Pure managed Collada (.dae) loader using System.Xml.Linq. No native dependencies required. Handles the subset of Collada produced by BeamNG's mesh export.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaLoader.LoadTextureMap(System.String)
member.summary: Parses the Collada material chain and returns a dictionary mapping each geometry primitive's material binding symbol to the texture image filename (e.g. "sunburst_body.png"). The file path stored in the DAE may be absolute from another machine — only the filename component is returned so callers can do a local lookup.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.DtsLoader
member.summary: Best-effort Torque DTS mesh loader. Supports static geometry extraction for common DTS v24-v26 files.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledNode
member.summary: A single assembled node: a named point-mass in the vehicle graph. Position is in BeamNG coordinate space (Y-forward, Z-up).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledNode.#ctor(System.String,System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Single,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.Boolean)
member.summary: A single assembled node: a named point-mass in the vehicle graph. Position is in BeamNG coordinate space (Y-forward, Z-up).

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledBeam
member.summary: A single assembled beam connecting two nodes.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledBeam.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A single assembled beam connecting two nodes.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledFlexBody
member.summary: A named group of mesh objects and the node groups they are driven by. Position is the absolute BeamNG-space origin of the mesh (when provided in the flexbody row).

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.AssembledFlexBody.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.Nullable{System.Numerics.Vector3},System.Nullable{System.Numerics.Vector3},System.Nullable{System.Numerics.Vector3},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A named group of mesh objects and the node groups they are driven by. Position is the absolute BeamNG-space origin of the mesh (when provided in the flexbody row).

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehiclePart
member.summary: A logical car part derived from a jbeam slot, containing all its nodes and mesh info. Parts marked Detachable become separate physics bodies with a breakable weld.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehiclePart.Detachable
member.summary: True when this part should become a separate physics body that can detach.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehiclePart.ExclusiveNodeIds
member.summary: Node IDs that belong exclusively to this part (not shared with chassis).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehiclePart.BreakStrength
member.summary: The minimum beamStrength of all beams connecting this part to the rest of the car. This is the force threshold at which it detaches.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehiclePart.FlexBodies
member.summary: Mesh objects driven by this part's node groups.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition
member.summary: A fully assembled vehicle definition produced by . All nodes, beams, and parts from the slot hierarchy are merged into flat collections. Coordinates are in BeamNG space: X = left, Y = forward, Z = up.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Nodes
member.summary: All nodes keyed by ID.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Beams
member.summary: All beams in the assembled vehicle.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.FlexBodies
member.summary: All flex-body mesh mappings.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Parts
member.summary: Logical parts (chassis + detachable pieces). The first entry (index 0) is always the main chassis.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.FolderPath
member.summary: Path to the vehicle folder (used for loading meshes).

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.RefNodes
member.summary: Orientation ref nodes from jbeam refNodes. Keys: "ref", "back", "left", "up", "leftCorner", "rightCorner".

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Vars
member.summary: Physics variables from the active JBeam variable defaults plus any loaded .pc overrides. Variable names are stripped of their leading '$' (e.g., "spring_F_asphalt" = 60000). Used by to set spring, damping, etc.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.PowertrainDevices
member.summary: Powertrain device graph assembled from active parts.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.PressureWheels
member.summary: Pressure wheel metadata assembled from active parts.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.PressureWheelOptions
member.summary: Pressure-wheel option objects assembled from the active parts.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.PartGearRatios
member.summary: Top-level gear ratios defined by active selected parts such as final drives.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Engine
member.summary: Engine metadata parsed from the active JBeam parts, when present.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.Gearbox
member.summary: Gearbox metadata parsed from the active JBeam parts, when present.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.VehicleController
member.summary: Shift and launch metadata parsed from the active JBeam vehicleController sections, when present.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.BrakeControl
member.summary: Brake-controller metadata parsed from active parts, including ABS flags and targets.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.TractionControl
member.summary: Traction-control metadata parsed from active parts, when present.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition.FuelTank
member.summary: Fuel-tank metadata parsed from active parts (capacity, starting fuel).

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader
member.summary: High-level vehicle loader.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.Load(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Loads the vehicle at , builds physics and mesh entities, and returns the assembled .
member.param: Absolute path to the vehicle folder containing .jbeam files.
Optional .pc config file name (e.g. "rally_pro_asphalt.pc") or base name without extension. If null, auto-detects: prefers rally_pro_asphalt.pc, then first .pc file found.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.BuildMeshEntity(System.Collections.Generic.List{LibreRally.Vehicle.Rendering.ColladaMesh},System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String},System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Groups sub-meshes by material symbol; for each group merges geometry into one GPU buffer and resolves a texture via three-level lookup: 1. BeamNG *.materials.json (full path, most accurate) 2. Collada library_images chain (filename in Textures/) 3. Grey placeholder (unresolvable, e.g. sunburst base-car textures not in the mod pack)

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.TryLoadTextureFromPath(System.String)
member.summary: Loads a texture from an absolute file path.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.TryLoadTexture(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Looks up a texture by filename in the vehicle folder (root or Textures/ subfolder). may already include an extension (from Collada map) or be a base name. Handles BeamNG DAE files that reference .color.png names when actual assets are .color.dds.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.BuildFallbackChassisModel(LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition)
member.summary: Creates a bright orange box model sized to the chassis AABB as a fallback mesh.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.EstimateWheelbase(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleBuilderResult)
member.summary: Estimates the wheelbase (front-to-rear axle distance in metres) from wheel entity positions. Falls back to a typical rally car wheelbase if positions are not yet available.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.EstimateTrackWidth(LibreRally.Vehicle.Physics.VehicleBuilderResult)
member.summary: Estimates the track width (left-to-right wheel distance in metres) from wheel entity positions.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoader.EstimateVehicleMass(LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleDefinition)
member.summary: Estimates vehicle mass from the total node weights in the definition.

member.summary: A loaded vehicle with its root entity and assembled definition.

member.name: M:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleLoadDiagnostics.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Single)
member.summary: A loaded vehicle with its root entity and assembled definition.

member.name: T:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript
member.summary: Handles keyboard driving input for a vehicle assembled by and applies drive and brake impulses to the chassis body, tracks steering input, and performs roll stabilization.

member.summary: Maximum forward drive impulse scaling applied while accelerating.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.BrakeTorque
member.summary: Braking impulse scaling applied when the brake or handbrake is engaged.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.MaxSteerAngle
member.summary: Maximum steering angle in radians.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.SteerSpeed
member.summary: Rate at which steering angle increases when steering input is held.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.SteerReturnSpeed
member.summary: Rate at which steering angle returns toward center when input is released.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.MaxSpeedMs
member.summary: Maximum chassis speed in metres per second at which engine impulse is applied.

member.name: P:LibreRally.Vehicle.VehicleScript.Definition
member.summary: Set by after assembly.
